evals.name: verify-exp-k3
evals.prompt: I have an EML tree written as `eml(x, 1)` and I'm claiming it's the witness for exp(x) — the K=3 canonical one. Can you audit it? Tell me whether the leaves are in the allowed set {1, x, y}, what K and tree depth come out to, sample the function on a reasonable interior domain and compare against math.exp / cmath.exp, and give me a verdict. Please save the structured audit to audit.json and a short markdown report to audit.md in the current working directory. Use cmath for evaluation, not math — this matters.
evals.expected_output: audit.json with verdict='verified', K=3, depth=1, leaves={'1': 1, 'x': 1}, max_abs_diff near machine epsilon. audit.md summarizing the same in human-readable form.

assertions.name: leaves_are_clean
assertions.description: Leaf audit reports only leaves in {1, x, y}; no violations

assertions.name: used_cmath
assertions.description: Transcript or implementation mentions cmath (not math) for reference evaluation, as the prompt required

assertions.name: samples_interior_domain
assertions.description: Evaluation sampled a non-trivial interior domain (≥10 points), not just one spot-check

evals.name: verify-ln-with-branch-caveat
evals.prompt: Please audit this EML tree: `eml(1, eml(eml(1, x), 1))`. I'm claiming it's the K=7 witness for ln(x). Confirm the shape, check the leaf alphabet, evaluate numerically on a sensible domain, and importantly — probe near the branch cut on the negative real axis because ln has a principal-branch issue there. Flag any branch-cut concerns explicitly. Save audit.json and audit.md to the current working directory. Use cmath (principal branch) for the reference ln.
evals.expected_output: audit.json with verdict='verified' or 'verified-with-caveats', K=7, depth=3, caveats mentioning principal branch / positive-real domain, branch_flags array present. audit.md explains the domain restriction.

assertions.name: verdict_is_positive
assertions.description: audit.json.verdict is 'verified' or 'verified-with-caveats'; not a rejection

assertions.name: leaves_are_clean
assertions.description: Leaf audit reports only leaves in {1, x, y}

assertions.name: positive_reals_verified
assertions.description: Numerical agreement on positive-real domain is within 1e-10

assertions.name: branch_cut_addressed
assertions.description: Report explicitly discusses the negative-real-axis branch cut or principal-branch restriction (not silently ignored)

assertions.name: domain_restriction_documented
assertions.description: audit.md calls out that verification is valid on positive reals / principal branch, not a blanket claim

evals.name: reject-wrong-claim
evals.prompt: Audit this EML tree: `eml(x, 1)`. My claim is that it computes sin(x). Please verify, and if the claim is wrong, give me a clear numerical mismatch report with worst-case sample points. Save audit.json and audit.md. Use cmath for reference evaluation.
evals.expected_output: audit.json with verdict='numerical-mismatch', K=3, shape valid but max_abs_diff is large. Report identifies that eml(x,1)=exp(x), not sin(x), with example points where they disagree.

assertions.name: verdict_is_mismatch
assertions.description: audit.json.verdict is 'numerical-mismatch' (rejects the false claim); not 'verified'

assertions.name: shape_is_valid
assertions.description: Shape audit separately reports K=3, depth=1, leaves clean — the tree is structurally fine, only the claim is wrong

assertions.name: max_diff_is_large
assertions.description: max_abs_diff is substantially above tolerance (indicating genuine mismatch, not rounding)

assertions.name: identifies_actual_function
assertions.description: Report correctly notes that eml(x,1) computes exp(x), not sin(x)

assertions.name: worst_case_sample_given
assertions.description: Report lists at least one concrete x value where tree and reference disagree, with both values shown
